title: paeonia corallina subsp. coriacea
intro: Paeonia corallina subsp. coriacea, also known as a type of peony, is a perennial plant native to China, characterized by its vibrant pink flowers and deep green foliage. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to soothe and calm the body. The health benefits of Paeonia corallina subsp. coriacea include its ability to reduce menstrual cramps, alleviate symptoms of menopause, and promote relaxation and sleep. In herbal preparations, the root of the plant is typically dried and used in teas, tinctures, or infusions, or combined with other herbs to create a remedy for a range of health issues.
